name: Fried Onion Mums or Pom Poms
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut off and discard the top half-inch of the onions.
2. Peel the onions but do not cut off the root end.
3. Place each onion, root end up, on a cutting board.
4. Make vertical cuts all around the onion about a quarter inch apart.
- For small onions, start the cut a quarter-inch from the root.
- For very large onions, start the cut a half-inch from the root.
5. Ensure the knife goes into the center of the onion.
6. Place all the onions in a large bowl of cold water with ice cubes; refrigerate several hours or overnight. The onions will open up like mums.
7. Drain upside-down when ready to proceed.
8. In a large bowl, mix together thoroughly the flour, baking powder, and spices.
9. In another bowl, beat together the eggs and milk.
10. Dip the onions one by one into the egg mixture, opening the petals with your fingers; let the excess drip off, and place in flour.
11. Work the flour mixture into the center gently with fingertips.
12. Shake off excess flour and repeat egg dip and flouring, shaking off the excess thoroughly.
13. Heat oil to 360°F or until a small piece of dry bread turns deep gold in 15 seconds.
14. Fry onions without crowding them—large ones one at a time; keep them submerged with a spatula or by placing the frying basket on top of them.
15. Plunge large onions into root end up and turn over once.
- To serve immediately, fry small onions for five minutes or large onions for six to seven minutes until deep gold.
16. Drain upside-down on paper towels; then invert on a serving plate. Keep warm in a low oven while frying the others.
17. Remove centers of large onions with a very sharp knife.
18. Small onions may be left whole, or the center can be scooped out with a melon-ball cutter using a small, sharp knife.
19. If serving the next day, fry until pale gold; do not fry completely.
20. When ready to serve, reheat oil to 380°F and fry just long enough to heat and brown about 15 seconds. Drain and serve.
21. For an alternative without making mums, cut onions vertically to make "petals" or crosswise and separate into rings. This can be done ahead, kept in ice water, and fried as above.
